The prompt asks if there will be at least one single day between the open date (likely mid-April 2025, based on the URL example) and June 1, 2025, where the words "tariff" or "tariffs" do not appear on the front page of BOTH the NYT and the WSJ. The base rate for a specific policy keyword appearing on the front page of at least one of two specific publications for 45+ consecutive days is extremely low. There would need to be an almost guaranteed structural reason (such as a specifically titled daily column, which searches did not find) or an unrelentingly severe daily news cycle centered exclusively on this issue without a single day's break—even on weekends when news is softer—for the word to appear on at least one front page every day. The simple math of probability (e.g., if the chance of the word appearing in at least one paper is an incredibly high 90% per day, a 45-day streak only has about a 0.8% chance of surviving) overwhelmingly favors a YES resolution.
